text: Arthur Hayes Says AI Has Drained Bitcoin Liquidity; Sold HYPE, NEAR, WLD and ZEC, May Use Derivatives for Tactical Shorts BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes said AI has absorbed a large share of newly created dollar liquidity, which he believes is a key reason Bitcoin has failed to rally further despite broader liquidity expansion. He argued that rising oil prices, supply pressure from major AI IPOs and U.S. political factors could jointly burst the AI stock bubble, dragging crypto lower in the short term before Bitcoin eventually rebounds on renewed liquidity. Hayes said Maelstrom sold HYPE, NEAR, WLD and ZEC last week, while retaining BTC and ETH, and may use derivatives for tactical shorts. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Highlight Clip The risk of total on-chain privacy exposure is extremely high(ARCHIVE FOOTAGE) On January 15, 2026, Vitalik Buterin warned about the lack of privacy on blockchains. Citing Zcash founder Zooko, he compared current blockchains to "Twitter for your bank account." Vitalik stated that without privacy protections, motivated individuals could easily build Twitter bots to automatically monitor and analyze your transactions, attempting to figure out the recipients and publicly post them. He believes this risk of privacy exposure is much closer to reality than people realize. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Humanity Protocol-Linked Wallets Drained for Over $19 Million, H Token Crashes More Than 80% JUST IN: Onchain analyst Specter reported that wallets linked to or previously interacting with Humanity Protocol may have been compromised. More than 17 wallets holding H tokens have been drained so far, with total losses exceeding $19 million. Funds were traced to multiple suspected attacker addresses. The root cause remains unknown, though affected wallets may share a common exposure related to Humanity Protocol. Following the incident, the H token plunged sharply, with intraday losses exceeding 80%. According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Humanity Protocol has suffered an exploit, with losses exceeding $31 million. The attacker is swapping Humanity Protocol's H token into ETH. — link

text: Anthropic: Paving the Way for Agents in Biology "We need to make biological data infrastructure more agent-friendly." > Anthropic researchers tasked scientific research agents (Claude, GPT, Biomni, and more) to retrieve viral sequence data from NCBI Virus, finding out that even top AI agents could not consistently achieve the level of accuracy required for reliable dataset construction. > However, accuracy rose to nearly 100% once a deterministic retrieval layer (gget virus) was added. > The broader conclusion is that biological databases need to be designed with agents in mind as users, and that deterministic retrieval tools are (currently) crucial to making agent workflows more reliable. Source:

text: State of the Themes: June 2026 Rising token costs and corporate budget pushback are shifting market attention from costly centralized cloud models toward efficiency. This trend is driving adoption of cheap open-source alternatives, digital observability infrastructure, and specialized edge hardware (like 3D NAND flash) to run localized inference without a heavy memory cost Physical automation is accelerating globally through custom silicon breakthroughs, such as dual-purpose color-sensing LiDAR chips. Simultaneously, global capex cycles are fueling growth outside of pure software, specifically benefiting European semiconductor enablers under the Chips Act 2.0, South Korean consumer luxury, right-wing policy shifts in Colombia, and traditional energy infrastructure pinched by structural oil inventory declines

text: TLDR • Strategy raised ~$181 million by selling new MSTR shares through their ATM (at-the-market) program • Used $101 million (55%) of that cash to buy 1,550 more BTC + other ~$80–100 million (45%) to boost their USD cash pile up to $1 billion • Dilution problem that people are worried: every time they sell new shares, the total number of MSTR shares outstanding increases (shareholders now own a smaller percentage of the company) -> i.e. printing new stock to buy more BTC Debate/questions • Question: is this now the permanent playbook? Keep ATM-ing shares forever, use roughly half for BTC and park the rest as cash? • It can pressure the stock price. More supply of shares = potential downward pressure if demand doesn't keep up • But if Bitcoin keeps going higher, dilution is "worth it" (the added BTC more than offsets the ownership loss). But if BTC just crabs or dips hard, shareholders get diluted and this ponzi falls • Saylor's view has always been: BTC is the best asset in the world. Issuing shares to buy more of it is accretive long-term because BTC appreciates faster than the dilution cost • MSTR basically became a leveraged Bitcoin proxy play, and the stock has done well historically because of this exact strategy • Just unclear if this half-BTC / half-cash approach is sustainable forever without eventually crushing existing shareholders

text: QCP Market Colour, 8 June 2026 Markets opened the week on edge. South Korea's KOSPI triggered circuit breakers after plunging 8.4%, as the AI trade came under pressure and foreign selling hit the country's semiconductor heavyweights. When chipmakers wobble in Korea, global risk tends to pay attention. For crypto, the mood remains defensive. BTC has found support above $60k after last week's sharp pullback, but options markets are still pricing caution, with front-end vol elevated and put skew firmly in demand. Macro is not helping either. Strong US jobs data, USD/JPY back above 160, Middle East tensions and this week's CPI release are all keeping investors on alert. Strategy remains another key swing factor, with uncertainty around STRC and dividend coverage still weighing on sentiment. So is BTC building a base above $60k, or is the market simply pausing before the next macro test? Key Events Wed, 10 Jun: US CPI Thu, 11 Jun: US PPI Fri, 12 Jun: SpaceX IPO, expected Read the full colour here.

text: JPMorgan said Strategy's recent sale of 32 BTC was small, but raised concerns over whether the company may continue selling Bitcoin to fund preferred stock dividends. The bank said Strategy's current dollar reserves cover only about 6.3 months of dividend payments, and shifted its view on digital assets from positive to cautious. JPMorgan said the second-half market outlook will depend on Strategy's plan to fund roughly $1.7 billion in annual dividends and whether the CLARITY Act can pass, with the bank now seeing the bill's chance of passing this year at below 50%. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Jiang Zhuoer, CEO of BTCTOP, said he believes Strategy is unlikely to significantly net-sell BTC. He argued that Strategy has little incentive to break its market image of "never selling BTC"; even if BTC falls to $30,000, its leverage ratio would only rise from about 5% to around 10%. He also said Strategy's STRC interest coverage logic is financially consistent: selling early low-cost BTC can generate accounting gains to pay STRC interest, while new STRC proceeds can be used to continue buying BTC, maintaining the net-buying narrative. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: According to Lookonchain, an Ethereum OG sold 60,000 ETH worth about $117.25 million and 9,442 wstETH worth about $24 million before the market crash, at an average price of $2,040. text: Famous trader Eugene said he has largely exited the crypto market and shifted to U.S. equities, saying the current crypto market lacks attractive risk-reward opportunities. He also said he is bearish on Strategy and Michael Saylor, arguing that the model may be starting to unravel. text: Yuga Labs CEO Michael Figge said the team has completed a whitehat rescue operation for an exploit discovered in Flooring Protocol. The rescued NFTs are now safely in Yuga Labs' custody, including 29 BAYC, 4 MAYC, 1 BAKC, 2 CryptoPunks, 1 Azuki, 2 Elementals, 26 Captains, 1 Moonbird and 2 Doodles. Yuga Labs said it instructed its GrailsOTC trading desk to front the funds and NFTs needed to rescue the at-risk assets, and will work with the protocol developers to return the assets once a solution is finalized. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Syscoin said in a preliminary postmortem that its Bridge has been paused after a security incident involving about 5B SYS. According to Syscoin, an attacker exploited a validation issue in the Bridge flow, causing the system to incorrectly accept or interpret a transaction proof and create about 5B unauthorized SYS outputs through the UTXO Bridge path. The funds were moved and split after reaching the UTXO chain, with about 4B SYS and 1B SYS linked to two major tainted addresses. Syscoin said it has identified the affected validation path, prepared a fix, and is coordinating with exchanges and ecosystem partners to blacklist, freeze or monitor SYS deposits connected to the tainted UTXO trail. Users are advised not to interact with the Bridge while it remains paused. — link
